expectations.text: Agent correctly identifies P0054 as OLD_PROJECT (the one to check for misrouted exchanges)
expectations.evidence: Step 1 states: 'OLD_PROJECT: P0054 (currently active)' and 'OLD_O: O0054 (corresponding O-entry for P0054)'

expectations.text: Agent calls load_project on P0048 (the target)
expectations.evidence: Step 2 states: 'MCP call I would make: load_project(id="P0048")'

expectations.text: Agent checks O0054 specifically for today's exchanges that may belong to the P0048 context
expectations.evidence: Step 3 states: 'MCP call I would make: read_memory(id="O0054")' and 'Filter for nodes with today's timestamp (2026-04-25)' and 'Read each exchange's content to determine if it discusses P0048 topics'

expectations.text: Agent mentions or describes using move_nodes to correct misrouted exchanges if found
expectations.evidence: Step 3 states: 'I would collect their node IDs at the batch level (L3) and call: move_nodes(node_ids: ["O0054.3"], target_o_id: "O0048")'

claims.claim: The agent provides both a 'no misrouting' and 'misrouting found' confirmation example
claims.evidence: Step 4 shows Scenario A (no misrouting) and Scenario B (misrouting with 1 batch moved) confirmation outputs

suggestions.assertion: Agent checks O0054 specifically for today's exchanges that may belong to the P0048 context
suggestions.reason: The assertion is satisfied by mentioning read_memory(O0054) and filtering by today's timestamp, but doesn't verify that the agent correctly distinguishes P0054 topics from P0048 topics within those exchanges. A response that just lists all today's exchanges without content-based classification would also pass this assertion.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are well-targeted for the misrouting-fix scenario. Good coverage of the key differentiating behaviors.
